title: Brandl et al. 2020 — Motor Imagery Under Distraction: An Open Access BCI Dataset
description: An open-access electroencephalography dataset of motor imagery brain-computer interface performance under six different distraction conditions. Sixteen healthy participants performed left versus right hand motor imagery while exposed to flickering video, number search tasks, news listening, eyes closed, vibro-tactile stimulation, or no distraction. The dataset comprises 504 trials per subject recorded at 1000 Hz across 63 EEG channels, with online classification using Common Spatial Patterns and Linear Discriminant Analysis.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired at 1000 Hz using 63 channels (10-20 montage) with nose reference via two BrainAmp amplifiers (Brain Products). Participants completed one calibration run (72 trials, no feedback/distraction) and six feedback runs (72 trials each) under different distraction conditions. Auditory cues indicated left or right hand motor imagery tasks with 4.5 s trial duration and 2.5 s inter-trial interval. Online classification employed Common Spatial Patterns with Linear Discriminant Analysis.
